components.title: @State
components.summary: A property wrapper for transient, view-owned source-of-truth value-type state that re-renders the view on change.
components.higNotes: Use @State only for transient UI state that the view itself owns (a toggle, a sheet flag, an editing string). Mark it private. For shared or model data use @Observable + @State-held reference, or pass a Binding. Never use @State to mirror a value that has another owner.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The stored source of truth. In a showroom expose a representative editable value (a Bool driven by toggle, an Int by stepper, a String by text).

properties.name: valueKind
properties.notes: Drives which demo control renders. Bool→Toggle, Int→Stepper, Double→Slider, String→TextField, Date→DatePicker.

properties.name: isPrivate
properties.notes: House convention: @State should always be declared private. Toggle is documentation-only; emit `private` keyword when on.

properties.name: initialValueExpression
properties.notes: Literal initializer used in the generated declaration; also usable with State(initialValue:) for computed defaults in init.

components.title: @Binding
components.summary: A two-way reference to a source of truth owned elsewhere, letting a child view read and mutate the parent's state.
components.higNotes: Pass a Binding to a child when the child needs to mutate a value the parent owns. Obtain it with the $ projected value of @State/@Bindable/@Observed. Prefer passing a focused Binding to a single field over passing the whole model when only one property is edited.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The value read/written through the binding; control type follows the wrapped value type.

properties.name: source
properties.notes: How the binding is constructed: $state projection, Binding.constant(_:), Binding(get:set:), or $bindable.property.

properties.name: useConstant
properties.notes: When on, generate Binding.constant(value) for previews/disabled demos — read-only, writes are ignored.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: Use binding.animation(_:) to animate downstream changes triggered through the binding.

components.title: @Observable (Observation macro)
components.summary: A macro that makes a reference type observable so SwiftUI tracks per-property reads and updates only views that depend on changed properties.
components.higNotes: Prefer @Observable over the legacy ObservableObject/@Published for all new model types. SwiftUI tracks individual property access, so views invalidate only on the specific properties they read. Hold the instance with @State for a view-owned model, @Environment for an injected one, or let for an external reference.

properties.name: ownershipPattern
properties.notes: How the observable instance reaches the view: @State private var model = Model(); @Environment(Model.self); plain let model: Model; or Model.shared.

properties.name: isMainActor
properties.notes: House convention: annotate UI-facing observable services @MainActor. Emits @MainActor before @Observable.

properties.name: ignoredProperties
properties.notes: Properties marked @ObservationIgnored are excluded from change tracking (caches, derived non-UI fields).

properties.name: trackedProperty
properties.notes: Demo property the view reads; mutating it invalidates only views observing it.

components.title: @Bindable
components.summary: A property wrapper that creates bindings to the mutable properties of an @Observable object, including ones injected via @Environment.
components.higNotes: Use @Bindable to get $-prefixed bindings into an Observable model's properties. As a stored property when the model is passed in, or as a local `@Bindable var x = x` inside body (or a ForEach element) to bind into a @State/@Environment-held observable. Don't use it to own the model — that's @State's job.

properties.name: usage
properties.notes: @Bindable var model: Model (passed in); @Bindable var model = model (local); or bridging an @Environment(Model.self) value to bindings.

properties.name: boundProperty
properties.notes: Which model property a control binds to via $model.<property>.

properties.name: controlForProperty
properties.notes: Demo control wired to the bound property.

components.title: @Environment (key path)
components.summary: Reads a built-in or custom EnvironmentValues entry by key path, propagated implicitly down the view hierarchy.
components.higNotes: Use @Environment to read ambient configuration (color scheme, dynamic type size, locale, dismiss action, scene phase) rather than threading it through initializers. Reading an environment value creates a dependency: the view invalidates when that value changes. Prefer the most specific built-in value over inventing a custom one.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: The environment key path to read. Showroom should let you pick any built-in EnvironmentValues key. Some are read-only actions (dismiss, openURL, refresh).

properties.name: readByType
properties.notes: iOS 17+: @Environment(Model.self) reads an injected Observable by type instead of by key path. When on, generate the type-based form.

properties.name: observableType
properties.notes: Type used with @Environment(Type.self) when readByType is on. Optional variant @Environment(Type.self) yields Type? if not injected.

components.title: EnvironmentValues
components.summary: The collection of all environment values flowing through a view hierarchy; the keyed store @Environment reads and the environment modifiers write.
components.higNotes: Treat EnvironmentValues as the canonical place to look up ambient state. Extend it with @Entry for custom values. Don't overload the environment with model data better passed explicitly; reserve it for cross-cutting configuration consumed deep in the tree.

properties.name: builtInValueGroup
properties.notes: Categorizes the built-in values for browsing: appearance (colorScheme, tint), accessibility (reduceMotion, sizeCategory), layout (sizeClass, layoutDirection), localization (locale, calendar, timeZone), actions (dismiss, openURL, openWindow, refresh), lifecycle (scenePhase), data (modelContext, managedObjectContext).

properties.name: subscriptAccess
properties.notes: Custom values read/written via values[MyKey.self] when not using @Entry.

components.title: .environment(_:_:) (key path writer)
components.summary: Sets a value for a writable EnvironmentValues key path on a view's subtree.
components.higNotes: Override an environment value for a whole subtree from the container. Prefer purpose-built modifiers (.foregroundStyle, .font, .environment(\.locale, ...)) where they exist. Setting at the right level avoids re-specifying configuration on every descendant.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Writable key path to override. Read-only action values (dismiss, openURL) cannot be set here.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The value to inject; type follows the key path's value type.

components.title: .environment(_:) (Observable injector)
components.summary: Injects an @Observable object into the environment so descendants read it by type with @Environment(Type.self).
components.higNotes: This is the modern replacement for .environmentObject. Inject Observable model objects at the root of the subtree that needs them. Reading by type keeps call sites clean and avoids fatal crashes from missing keys when you use the optional read form.

properties.name: object
properties.notes: The Observable instance to inject. Hold it with @State at the injection site so it persists across body re-evaluations.

properties.name: allowNilInjection
properties.notes: Optional overload .environment(value as Model?) injects an optional, so descendants reading @Environment(Model.self) get nil instead of crashing when absent.

components.title: .transformEnvironment(_:transform:)
components.summary: Mutates an existing environment value in place for a subtree, deriving the new value from the inherited one.
components.higNotes: Use when the new value depends on the inherited one (append to a list, scale a number, clamp a size). Prefer .environment(_:_:) when you simply set an absolute value.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Key path of the value to transform.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: Closure mutating the inherited value in place.

components.title: EnvironmentKey
components.summary: The protocol that defines a custom environment value's default — backs custom EnvironmentValues entries (manual alternative to @Entry).
components.higNotes: Define custom environment values only for genuinely cross-cutting configuration consumed far from where it's set. Prefer the @Entry macro (iOS 13+) for new code — it generates the key and accessor for you. Use the manual EnvironmentKey conformance only when you need a named key type for reuse.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Required static defaultValue returned when no ancestor sets the value.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: The associated Value type. Reference-type values should be sendable/immutable to be safe across the tree.

properties.name: accessorName
properties.notes: Computed property added to EnvironmentValues to expose the key by name.

components.title: @Entry
components.summary: A macro that generates a custom entry for EnvironmentValues, Transaction, ContainerValues, or FocusedValues in one line.
components.higNotes: Preferred modern way to add custom environment/transaction/container/focused values. For EnvironmentValues/Transaction/ContainerValues you supply a default. For FocusedValues the type must be Optional and the default is always nil.

properties.name: targetStructure
properties.notes: Which value collection to extend. FocusedValues entries must be Optional with no explicit default.

properties.name: name
properties.notes: Name of the generated property.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Stored type. For FocusedValues must be an Optional.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Default for Environment/Transaction/Container entries; omitted (always nil) for FocusedValues.

components.title: @AppStorage
components.summary: A property wrapper that reads and writes a UserDefaults value and re-renders the view when that default changes.
components.higNotes: Use @AppStorage for small, simple, app-wide preferences (theme, onboarding-seen, toggles). Never store sensitive data or large blobs in UserDefaults. House convention: model singleton services persist richer state via JSON in UserDefaults rather than scattering @AppStorage across views.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: UserDefaults key. Keep keys namespaced and stable across releases.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: Default value if the key is absent. Supported: Bool, Int, Double, String, URL, Data, and RawRepresentable with Int/String raw value (plus their Optionals).

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Constrains the supported storage types and which demo control renders.

properties.name: store
properties.notes: Optional UserDefaults suite (e.g. App Group for widget sharing). Defaults to the value of .defaultAppStorage or .standard.

components.title: .defaultAppStorage(_:)
components.summary: Sets the UserDefaults store that @AppStorage uses by default for a view subtree.
components.higNotes: Use to point a subtree's @AppStorage at an App Group suite (shared with widgets/extensions) without specifying store: on every wrapper. Apply once near the root of the feature that shares defaults.

properties.name: store
properties.notes: The defaults suite to use for all descendant @AppStorage without an explicit store:.

components.codeTemplate:
SettingsView()
    .defaultAppStorage(UserDefaults(suiteName: {suiteName})!)

components.title: @SceneStorage
components.summary: A property wrapper for lightweight per-scene state that the system automatically saves and restores across app launches.
components.higNotes: Use @SceneStorage for UI restoration state scoped to a scene (selected tab, scroll position, draft text). Each scene has its own storage; data isn't shared between scenes and is destroyed when the scene is. Keep it lightweight — never store model data or sensitive data here.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Identifier unique within the scene's storage.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: Default value; supported types match AppStorage (Bool, Int, Double, String, URL, Data, RawRepresentable Int/String, and Optionals).

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Constrains storable types and the demo control.

components.title: @FocusState
components.summary: A property wrapper that reads and programmatically moves keyboard/system focus among focusable views in a scene.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusState to drive and observe focus: advance fields on submit, focus the first invalid field on validation, or dismiss the keyboard by setting it to nil/false. The wrapped value must be Bool or an Optional Hashable. Avoid binding the same value to two fields (ambiguous) — use distinct enum cases.

properties.name: valueShape
properties.notes: Bool form for a single focusable view; Optional Hashable (often an enum) to track which of several fields is focused.

properties.name: fieldEnum
properties.notes: The Hashable type enumerating focusable targets when using the optional form.

properties.name: boundModifier
properties.notes: focused(_:) binds a Bool to whether the view (or a descendant) has focus; focused(_:equals:) matches a specific enum case for granular control.

properties.name: programmaticFocus
properties.notes: Set the wrapped value to move focus; set to nil/false to resign focus and dismiss the keyboard.

components.title: .focused(_:) / .focused(_:equals:)
components.summary: Modifiers that associate a focusable view with a @FocusState binding, by Boolean presence or by matching a specific value.
components.higNotes: Attach .focused(_:equals:) to each field with its distinct case for multi-field forms; use .focused(_:) with a Bool for a single field or to observe whether a container or its descendants hold focus.

properties.name: binding
properties.notes: Projected value of a @FocusState property.

properties.name: equalsValue
properties.notes: The value this view's focus corresponds to; omitted in the Bool overload.

components.codeTemplate:
TextField("Field", text: $text)
    .focused({binding}, equals: {equalsValue})

components.title: \.isFocused (Environment)
components.summary: A read-only environment value indicating whether the nearest focusable ancestor currently has focus.
components.higNotes: Read \.isFocused to restyle a control (highlight, scale) based on whether its focus scope is active — common on tvOS for focus-driven UI and on macOS/iPad with hardware keyboards.

properties.name: isFocused
properties.notes: Read-only; reflects focus of the enclosing focusable region. Pair with .focusable() to define the region.

components.title: @FocusedValue
components.summary: Reads a value published by the currently focused view or an ancestor, reflecting the value closest to focus.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusedValue (with .focusedValue/.focusedSceneValue) to wire commands, menu bar items, and toolbar actions to whatever document or selection is focused — central to macOS multi-window apps. The wrapped value is always Optional (nil when nothing focused publishes the key).

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Custom FocusedValues entry (declared with @Entry, Optional type). iOS 17+/macOS 14+ also support @FocusedValue(MyType.self).

properties.name: readByType
properties.notes: Type-based read @FocusedValue(Type.self) introduced alongside the @Entry-driven keying; otherwise read by key path.

components.title: @FocusedBinding
components.summary: Reads a Binding published by the focused view, unwrapping it so commands can both read and write the focused view's value.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusedBinding when a command needs to mutate (not just read) a value owned by the focused view — e.g. a menu toggle that flips a property of the focused document. Publish with .focusedSceneValue(_:) passing a Binding.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: FocusedValues entry whose value is a Binding. The wrapper unwraps it to an Optional Value with two-way access.

components.title: .focusedValue / .focusedSceneValue
components.summary: Publishes a value or binding into FocusedValues so focused-value readers (commands, menus) can observe it.
components.higNotes: Use .focusedValue when the value should be observed only while the modified view is focused; use .focusedSceneValue to publish at scene scope (the active window), which is what menu-bar commands typically read.

properties.name: scope
properties.notes: View-focus scope vs. scene (active-window) scope.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: FocusedValues entry to publish into.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Value (or Binding) to publish. Pass $binding when paired with @FocusedBinding readers.

components.codeTemplate:
DocumentView()
    .{scope}({keyPath}, {value})

components.title: PreferenceKey
components.summary: A protocol defining a typed value that flows up the view tree from descendants to ancestors, with a reduce strategy for conflicts.
components.higNotes: Use a PreferenceKey to report data from children to a container (measured sizes, a title, a list of section anchors). Provide a defaultValue and a reduce(value:nextValue:) that merges contributions from multiple subviews. Prefer built-in mechanisms (navigationTitle, layout) when they exist.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: The Value associated type. Common: CGFloat (max width), [Anchor<CGRect>], String.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Value used when no view sets the preference.

properties.name: reduceStrategy
properties.notes: How reduce(value:nextValue:) combines multiple subview contributions.

components.title: .preference(key:value:)
components.summary: Sets a value for a custom PreferenceKey on a view, contributing it upward to ancestor readers.
components.higNotes: Attach .preference to the descendant that knows the value (often inside a background GeometryReader for sizes). Multiple contributions get merged by the key's reduce.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: The preference key type.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Value to contribute. Frequently derived from a GeometryReader in a background.

components.title: .onPreferenceChange(_:perform:)
components.summary: Runs a closure in the container whenever the reduced value for a PreferenceKey changes.
components.higNotes: Read aggregated child preferences in the parent here, typically copying into @State to drive layout. Avoid heavy work in the closure; the Value must be Equatable. Beware update loops if the state you set changes the preference.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: The key to observe (its Value must be Equatable).

properties.name: perform
properties.notes: Closure receiving the new reduced value; usually stores it into @State.

components.title: .transformPreference(_:_:)
components.summary: Modifies the value of a PreferenceKey in place as it propagates up, deriving it from the current value.
components.higNotes: Use to adjust a preference contribution mid-tree (offset a measured value, tag an entry) without replacing it outright. Prefer .preference when setting an absolute value.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Key whose propagating value to transform.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: In-place mutation of the inherited preference value.

components.title: .anchorPreference(key:value:transform:)
components.summary: Reports a geometric Anchor (bounds, point) up the tree via a PreferenceKey, resolved later in a coordinate space.
components.higNotes: Use anchor preferences for overlays/backgrounds that must align to a descendant's frame regardless of layout nesting (tooltips, selection highlights, connecting lines). Resolve the Anchor with a GeometryProxy in overlayPreferenceValue/backgroundPreferenceValue.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Preference key whose Value holds Anchor data.

properties.name: anchorSource
properties.notes: Which geometry to capture as the anchor.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: Maps the captured anchor into the key's Value (often wrapping in an array or dictionary).

components.title: .overlayPreferenceValue / .backgroundPreferenceValue
components.summary: Builds an overlay or background using the reduced value of a PreferenceKey, with a GeometryProxy to resolve anchors.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 (alignment overload: iOS 18.0+)
components.higNotes: Use these to draw decorations positioned from child anchors collected via preferences — selection rings, callouts, badges. Resolve Anchor values with proxy[anchor]. The iOS 18 alignment overloads let you align the generated layer.

properties.name: layer
properties.notes: Whether the generated content sits above (overlay) or below (background) the view.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Key whose reduced value feeds the builder.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Available on the iOS 18+ alignment overloads to position the generated layer.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: View builder receiving the preference value; use a GeometryReader/proxy to resolve anchors to rects.

components.codeTemplate:
ContainerView()
    .{layer}PreferenceValue({key}) { value in
        GeometryReader { proxy in
            let rect = proxy[value]
            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8)
                .stroke()
                .frame(width: rect.width, height: rect.height)
                .offset(x: rect.minX, y: rect.minY)
        }
    }

components.title: .onChange(of:initial:_:)
components.summary: Runs an action when an observed Equatable value changes, optionally firing once on initial appearance, with old/new values.
components.minOS: iOS 17.0 / iPadOS 17.0 / macOS 14.0 / tvOS 17.0 / watchOS 10.0 (current two-parameter form)
components.higNotes: Use onChange to react to value changes — but prefer deriving state during body or using task(id:) for async work. The iOS 17 form gives both old and new values; the zero/two-parameter and initial: variants replaced the deprecated single-parameter API.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The value to observe; must be Equatable.

properties.name: initial
properties.notes: When true, fires the action once when the view first appears, in addition to on change.

properties.name: closureShape
properties.notes: Action signature: () -> Void or (oldValue, newValue) -> Void.

components.codeTemplate:
TextField("Query", text: $query)
    .onChange(of: {value}, initial: {initial}) { {closureShape == "oldNew" ? "oldValue, newValue" : ""} in
        performSearch()
    }

components.title: .onReceive(_:perform:)
components.summary: Runs an action when a Combine Publisher emits a value, bridging external event streams into a view.
components.higNotes: Use onReceive to respond to Combine publishers — timers, NotificationCenter, system notifications. For modern Swift concurrency prefer .task with an AsyncSequence where possible. Keep the closure light and route work to a model.

properties.name: publisher
properties.notes: Any Combine Publisher with Failure == Never.

properties.name: perform
properties.notes: Closure receiving each emitted element.

components.title: @StateObject (legacy)
components.summary: A property wrapper that instantiates and owns an ObservableObject for a view's lifetime — the pre-Observation way to own reference model data.
components.higNotes: Legacy: for new code prefer @Observable + @State. Use @StateObject only when interoperating with existing ObservableObject types. It guarantees a single instance across body re-evaluations (unlike @ObservedObject).

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The owned ObservableObject; constructed in an autoclosure so it's created exactly once.

properties.name: publishedDriver
properties.notes: Demonstrates that @Published properties on the object trigger view updates.

components.title: @ObservedObject (legacy)
components.summary: Observes an externally-owned ObservableObject passed into a view — the pre-Observation way to receive a reference model.
components.higNotes: Legacy: prefer passing an @Observable object as a plain let in new code. @ObservedObject does not own its object — never initialize the source of truth here, or it will be recreated on each parent update. Use $ to derive bindings to its @Published properties.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The observed object, owned elsewhere (typically a @StateObject upstream).

properties.name: bindingProperty
properties.notes: A @Published property to bind via $model.<property>.

components.title: @EnvironmentObject / .environmentObject(_:) (legacy)
components.summary: Reads an ObservableObject injected into the environment by type — the pre-Observation counterpart to @Environment(Type.self).
components.higNotes: Legacy: prefer .environment(_:) + @Environment(Type.self) with @Observable. @EnvironmentObject crashes at runtime if no ancestor injected the object via .environmentObject(_:) — always inject before reading. Useful only when bridging existing ObservableObject code.

properties.name: objectType
properties.notes: The ObservableObject type read from the environment.

properties.name: injected
properties.notes: States: when no ancestor injects the object, reading it crashes — the 'error' state demonstrates this requirement.

components.title: @ScaledMetric
components.summary: A dynamic property that scales a numeric value relative to the current Dynamic Type size, keeping custom dimensions (icon sizes, paddings) proportional to the user's text-size setting.
components.minOS: iOS 14.0 / iPadOS 14.0 / macOS 11.0 / tvOS 14.0 / watchOS 7.0 / Mac Catalyst 14.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Use @ScaledMetric to make custom layout dimensions honor Dynamic Type — e.g. a thumbnail size or spacing that grows with the user's preferred text size. Optionally anchor the scaling to a specific text style with relativeTo:. It reads \.dynamicTypeSize from the environment, so it belongs in the data-flow story alongside @Environment. Prefer it over hardcoded CGFloats whenever a dimension should track text size for accessibility.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The base value at the default text size (Value: BinaryFloatingPoint, typically CGFloat). The wrapper returns this scaled to the current Dynamic Type size.

properties.name: relativeTo
properties.notes: Optional text style the scaling is relative to. Omit for default body-relative scaling; supply to match a specific style's scale curve.

components.title: @GestureState
components.summary: A property wrapper that holds transient state while a gesture is active and automatically resets to its initial value when the gesture ends.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 / Mac Catalyst 13.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Use @GestureState for state that should exist only for the duration of a gesture (drag translation, is-pressing flag). Update it via the gesture's updating(_:body:) callback; it auto-resets when the gesture becomes inactive, so you never write reset logic. For state that must persist after the gesture ends, use @State instead and update it in onEnded.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: Initial value the property resets to when the gesture is inactive. Common types: Bool (is-pressing), CGSize (drag translation), CGFloat (magnification).

properties.name: valueKind
properties.notes: Shape of the transient value: Bool for press detection, CGSize for drag offset, CGFloat for magnify/rotate amount.

components.title: @Namespace
components.summary: A dynamic property that vends a stable namespace ID tied to the view's persistent identity, used to link views across transitions (matchedGeometryEffect, glassEffectID).
components.minOS: iOS 14.0 / iPadOS 14.0 / macOS 11.0 / tvOS 14.0 / watchOS 7.0 / Mac Catalyst 14.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Declare @Namespace to obtain a Namespace.ID, then pass it to matchedGeometryEffect(id:in:) on the source and destination views so SwiftUI animates a shared element between layouts. The same namespace is used by glassEffectID (iOS 26) for morphing glass. The namespace is part of view data flow: it's created once per view identity and propagated to children.

properties.name: namespaceID
properties.notes: The projected ID value, read as the wrapped value. Pass it into matchedGeometryEffect(id:in:) (the `in:` argument).

properties.name: matchID
properties.notes: The id: passed to matchedGeometryEffect on both endpoints so they are recognized as the same element.

components.title: @FocusedObject (legacy)
components.summary: Reads an ObservableObject published by the focused view or an ancestor — the pre-Observation counterpart to @FocusedValue for reference-type models.
components.minOS: iOS 16.0 / iPadOS 16.0 / macOS 13.0 / tvOS 16.0 / watchOS 9.0 / Mac Catalyst 16.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Legacy: prefer @FocusedValue with @Observable models or @Entry-keyed FocusedValues in new code. Use @FocusedObject only when wiring menu-bar commands or toolbar actions to an existing ObservableObject owned by the focused scene/view (published via .focusedSceneObject(_:) / .focusedObject(_:)). The wrapped value is always Optional — nil when no focused view publishes the object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.notes: The ObservableObject type to read from the focused view. The wrapped value is Optional (nil when nothing focused publishes it).

properties.name: publishScope
properties.notes: How the object is published upstream: .focusedObject(_:) (view-focus scope) or .focusedSceneObject(_:) (active-window scope, typical for menu commands).
